meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Design with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional case studies fast.
content.answer_block: You don't need years of experience to land clients; you need a high-velocity proof of concept. By leveraging Antigravity for UI generation and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can build three production-grade, niche-specific SaaS components in a weekend, effectively simulating a seasoned portfolio that converts leads into paying clients.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the freelancer's greatest enemy. You have the skills, but without a track record, potential clients treat you like a risk rather than a solution. You are stuck in the 'no work, no portfolio' loop, watching opportunities slip away to those who simply have more to show.

Stop waiting for the perfect client to hand you a project. We are going to bridge that gap by synthesizing professional-grade assets using AI. By combining Antigravity’s rapid UI scaffolding with the raw execution power of Claude Code, you will transform your development speed into a competitive advantage that commands authority.

steps.title: Define Your Niche SaaS Prototype
steps.description: Select a high-intent niche (e.g., HVAC dispatching, dental CRM) and define the core problem your app solves. Clarity here prevents scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-fidelity landing page and dashboard layout for a niche SaaS tool targeting [Niche]. Focus on a clean, modern B2B aesthetic with high-conversion CTA placements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --prompt 'Setup a clean React/Tailwind project structure for a SaaS dashboard based on the provided Antigravity design specs.'

steps.title: Generate UI Components with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate clean, modular UI components that look like enterprise software. This ensures your code is production-ready, not just a mockup.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive navigation bar, a data-driven metrics card, and a user feedback table using React and Tailwind CSS. Ensure all components are accessible.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --prompt 'Incorporate the new components into the existing directory and ensure consistent state management.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic via Claude Code
steps.description: Don't just build a pretty face. Use Claude Code to write the backend logic or API integration that makes the app functional.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement a mock API integration using TanStack Query to fetch and display dynamic metrics on the dashboard.'

steps.title: Iterative Refinement and Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on the design based on real-world UX patterns. Speed is irrelevant if the UX is broken.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current UI to improve mobile responsiveness and add micro-interactions for button states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --prompt 'Run a visual regression test and fix any overflow issues on mobile screens.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: A portfolio project is useless if it's trapped on your machine. Deploy to Vercel or Netlify to share the live link immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide instructions for production-ready build configuration.

pro_tips: Always build for a specific persona: A generic dashboard is invisible, but a 'CRM for Dog Walkers' is a conversation starter.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you go; clean documentation signals to clients that you are a senior-level engineer.
Implement a 'Case Study' page within your app that explains the 'why' behind your design decisions to demonstrate business acumen.
Leverage Antigravity to generate variants of your UI so you can show clients you understand A/B testing and conversion optimization.
Keep your repository public but add a 'How this was built' README to showcase your technical process and AI-augmented workflow.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, record a 60-second Loom video walking through the features and share it on LinkedIn. Then, repeat the process for two more niches to build a versatile portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact workflow to stop being a beginner and start being a builder. Stop overthinking the lack of experience and start shipping the proof. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
